tasks.title: Cat food and water check
tasks.notes: Care for Harvey, Callie, and cat siblings as applicable.

tasks.title: Eat meal 1
tasks.notes: Minimum food care.

tasks.title: Hydrate
tasks.notes: Water/body support.

tasks.title: Take meds
tasks.notes: Daily medication/routine support for either person.

tasks.title: Decompression break
tasks.notes: Rest, sensory reset, or burnout prevention.

tasks.title: Dishes / kitchen reset
tasks.notes: Kitchen maintenance, with low-spoon versions allowed.

tasks.title: Eat meal 2
tasks.notes: Second meal or safe snack support.

tasks.title: Hygiene check
tasks.notes: Basic hygiene, comfort, and dignity.

tasks.title: Shower / bathe / wash-up
tasks.notes: Hygiene support with low-spoon alternatives allowed.

tasks.title: Use DBT skill
tasks.notes: Emotion regulation and coping.

tasks.title: Use ADHD skill card
tasks.notes: Support, reminders, grounding, and task initiation.

tasks.title: Diary card / daily check-in
tasks.notes: Mood, skill, pain, energy, or stress tracking.

tasks.title: Journal entry
tasks.notes: Processing thoughts and feelings.

tasks.title: Grocery delivery order
tasks.notes: Household food restock.

tasks.title: Cat supply inventory
tasks.notes: Track litter, food, treats, and care supplies.

tasks.title: Inventory what the house needs
tasks.notes: Prevent running out of important supplies.

tasks.title: Laundry step
tasks.notes: Start, switch, fold, sort, or put away one manageable step.

tasks.title: Go to doctor / appointment
tasks.notes: Healthcare access and follow-through.

tasks.title: Complete 1 paperwork task
tasks.notes: Forms, benefits, housing, medical, or household paperwork.

tasks.title: Make important phone call
tasks.notes: Life admin and communication.

tasks.title: Take trash out + replace bag
tasks.notes: Trash maintenance and sanitation.

tasks.title: Add shop link / past order / favorite store
tasks.notes: Store management for rewards and wish items.

tasks.title: Play game to decompress
tasks.notes: Regulated rest and recovery.

tasks.title: Watch comfort content
tasks.notes: Mood boost and decompression.

tasks.title: Clean shower — tiny steps
tasks.notes: Break into one micro-step at a time; stop before pain, dizziness, MCAS symptoms, or breathing strain spikes.
tinySteps: Choose one piece only: spray, rinse, wipe, or put supplies away.
Use ventilation/mask or unscented cleaner; Jasper avoids triggers.
William can do reachable planning/checkoff from wheelchair or bed if useful.
Pause between steps; finishing later still counts.

tasks.title: Clean toilet — tiny steps
tasks.notes: A small sanitation pass counts; do not turn this into a full bathroom reset.
tinySteps: Gather one safe cleaner and gloves.
Do bowl, seat, or outside surfaces as separate steps.
Sit, brace, or ask for help before bending/twisting.
Stop when symptoms say stop.

tasks.title: Therapy support check
tasks.notes: Make therapy easier, not bigger.
tinySteps: Check date/time for therapy or therapy homework.
Write one sentence about what needs support.
Put meds, water, stim/comfort item, and charger within reach.
Afterward, schedule a decompression block.

tasks.title: Trash — one bag or one bin
tasks.notes: Only one small trash step is required.
tinySteps: Tie one bag or empty one small bin.
Use wheelchair-accessible route or handoff.
Replace liner only if that is safe today.
Wash hands or sanitize afterward.

tasks.title: Laundry — one tiny step
tasks.notes: Any single laundry step counts.
tinySteps: Choose one: gather, start washer, move to dryer, fold five items, or put away five items.
Use baskets at reachable height.
No stairs/heavy loads without help.
Stop at one step if bodies are done.

tasks.title: Safe food plan
tasks.notes: Plan a few low-effort safe options for both bodies.
tinySteps: List two safe foods and one backup drink.
Check for trigger foods/smells before ordering or cooking.
Put easy items within reach.
Save one emergency fallback option.

tasks.title: Bathroom reset — micro version
tasks.notes: Monthly reset is split across tiny bathroom zones.
tinySteps: Pick only one zone: sink, shower shelf, toilet supplies, floor corner, or trash.
Use fragrance-safe products and ventilation.
Sit whenever possible; no twisting through scoliosis pain.
Leave the rest for another day.

tasks.title: Accessibility supplies check
tasks.notes: Check supplies that keep the house easier and safer.
tinySteps: Check wipes, gloves, masks, meds organizers, chargers, trash bags, and hydration supplies.
Add missing items to GiftLink or shopping list.
Move high-use items into reach zones.
Stop after the list; buying can be separate.

tasks.title: Therapy admin — light pass
tasks.notes: One small admin pass for appointments, paperwork, or goals.
tinySteps: Check upcoming therapy dates.
Write one therapy goal or one concern.
Save paperwork/photo/portal note if needed.
Schedule recovery time after hard calls.

tasks.title: Deep clean plan, not deep clean
tasks.notes: Plan the next tiny cleaning steps without forcing a cleaning marathon.
tinySteps: Name the one area causing the most stress.
Break it into five-minute pieces.
Assign only what each body can safely do.
Put the rest into weekly pulls.
